framework_name: Government Digital and Data Profession Capability Framework
publisher: Government Digital Service (GDS), Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), UK Government
version: Current as of 2026-03-23 (living framework, quarterly updates)
description: The UK Government's capability framework for digital, data, and technology professionals across the civil service. Defines 52 roles across 8 role families with 189 skills. Each skill has four proficiency levels (Awareness, Working, Practitioner, Expert). Originally published March 2017, continuously updated.
scope: 52 roles: Architecture (7), Chief digital and data (4), Data (9), IT operations (12), Product and delivery (6), QAT (3), Software development (3), User-centred design (8). 189 skills with proficiency level descriptions.
strm_relevance: DDaT is the UK equivalent of US DCWF — an authoritative government framework defining data/digital workforce competencies. Maps directly to WIDAI scope via Data roles (data architect, data engineer, data scientist, data governance manager, data ethicist, ML engineer, analytics engineer) and cross-cutting skills. Provides international validation of WIDAI KSA pool coverage.
notes: Framework was renamed from 'Digital, Data and Technology (DDaT) Profession Capability Framework' to current name on 1 December 2023 following launch of the Government Digital and Data brand. Source CSVs downloaded from official S3-hosted export.
